channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's changes to the federal government aren't yet a clear political winner or loser: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's dramatic changes to the federal government haven't emerged as an obvious political winner or loser, according to a new poll that indicates some Americans may be giving him the benefit of the doubt for now on his Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 GOP senators vote with Democrats to undo Trump tariffs on Canada
item.description: A group of Republican senators voted along with Democrats on Wednesday for a resolution to undo President Trump's 25 percent tariffs on Canadian imports, dealing an embarrassing blow to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Officer previously accused of excessive force confirmed for state board by Texas Senate
item.description: One Senate Democrat joined Republicans, providing the margin needed to approve Justin Berry to the state law-enforcement board.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 things to know about Republicans’ new blueprint to pass Trump's agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans on Wednesday rolled out a 70-page blueprint to advance President Trump’s tax agenda, along with directions that could pave the way for more than a trillion dollars in cuts to government spending.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: GOP defectors help Senate advance resolution to cancel Trump tariffs despite White House veto warning
item.description: The Senate voted on a resolution to undo Trump’s tariffs against Canada on Wednesday, and some Republicans went on record opposing the president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate budget blueprint empowers GOP chair to decide if Trump tax cuts add to deficit
item.description: Senate Republicans on Wednesday unveiled a 70-page budget resolution that they say would give Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.), a staunch ally of President Trump, the power to determine whether extending the 2017 Trump tax cuts officially adds to the federal deficit.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Tuesday’s elections, including bad news for Elon Musk
item.description: Democrats can take yesterday’s elections as one of the first bits of good news they’ve had in months. But there are lots of issues that are still center-right, including immigration.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump restoring millions in family planning funds for two states
item.description: The Trump administration is restoring millions of dollars in Title X funds to two states after the Biden administration chose to withhold those funds because the pair failed to comply with program rules.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Judge dismisses corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge dismissed New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case on Wednesday, agreeing it was the only practical outcome while blasting the Justice Department’s “troubling” rationale for wanting the charges thrown out — namely so the Democrat could help President Donald Tru
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace claimed the assault came in response to her efforts to bar transgender women from using women's restrooms on federal property.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, NewsNation

item.title: Celebrating fact-checking around the globe
item.description: It's International Fact-Checking Day, an event to highlight the work of fact-checkers around the world.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Law firms fear Trump orders could affect security clearances of lawyers who are military reservists
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says his executive orders targeting law firms are being issued in the name of national security, with the White House asserting that the firms don't deserve access to sensitive U.S. government information.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces sweeping 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced plans for sweeping tariffs Wednesday, saying “our country has been looted, pillaged, raped, plundered” by other nations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No bathroom breaks: How Booker accomplished record Senate speech
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) set a new record for the longest Senate speech in history on Monday and Tuesday by holding the upper chamber’s floor for more than 25 hours.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed cut in local aid to prevent aquatic invasive species sparks fears of more infested lakes
item.description: For the past decade, Minnesota has distributed about $10 million a year to counties for aquatic invasive species prevention. Gov. Tim Walz’s proposed budget reduced that amount by 50 percent. Some lake advocates worry cutting the funding could potentially lead to more infested waters.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams federal bribery case dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams' federal bribery case has been dismissed, court records show. In a letter filed Wednesday, Judge Dale Ho ordered the case dropped with prejudice, meaning the government cannot bring these charges against Adams again in the future.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Who is Susan Crawford? Wisconsin Supreme Court winner has fought for union and abortion rights
item.description: Wisconsin Supreme Court winner Susan Crawford has served as a Dane County Circuit Court judge since 2018. Crawford won election that year and again in 2022 to the seat. The county is home to Wisconsin’s liberal capital, Madison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Dem attorneys general use new media to mount Trump resistance
item.description: Court proceedings might often unfold behind closed doors, but the Democratic attorneys general challenging President Trump’s expansive agenda want to bring their work to your feed.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces sweeping new tariffs to promote US manufacturing, risking inflation and trade wars
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced far-reaching new tariffs on nearly all U.S. trading partners — including a 34% tax on imports from China and 20% on the European Union, among others.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California lawmakers reject bills to ban trans athletes' participation in girls sports
item.description: California lawmakers won't change state policies allowing transgender kids and teens to play on sports teams consistent with their gender identities.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Booker sets record for longest Senate speech in history
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Tuesday set a new record for longest floor speech in Senate history, having held the floor for 25 hours and 5 minutes to decry potential GOP spending cuts in their looming tax bill and policies put in place by the Trump administration. Booker, the No.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Results for key races in suburban elections
item.description: Municipal elections were held in the suburbs of Chicago, with key races including the Aurora mayoral race and Evanston mayor race, as well as a new mayor being elected in Dolton.
item.dc:creator: Anna Roberts, WGN 9

item.title: Legal, medical cannabis fix-it legislation in the works at Minnesota Legislature
item.description: Sen. Scott Dibble, DFL-Minneapolis, is sponsoring several of those cleanup bills for both recreational cannabis and changes to medical marijuana rules. He joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer ahead of committee hearings Thursday to talk about what the proposed alterations entail.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration cutting some funds to Planned Parenthood
item.description: Nine Planned Parenthood affiliates received notices about the Trump administration’s plans to withhold Title X funding starting April 1, according to the release.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Layoffs begin at U.S. health agencies responsible for research, tracking disease and regulating food
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department began receiving notices of dismissal Tuesday in an overhaul ultimately expected to lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court seems ready to let religious groups opt out of unemployment compensation laws
item.description: The case was brought by a chapter of Catholic Charities in Wisconsin, which says that it should be able to opt out of the mandatory state unemployment compensation system.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Hundreds protest the ICE detainment of a U of M graduate student
item.description: Protesters gathered at the University of Minnesota Twin Cities campus after an international graduate student was detained by immigration officials on Thursday. During the rally, it was announced that a student at Minnesota State University, Mankato was also detained.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Matt Sepic, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Legislation aimed at protecting Florida state parks stuck in committee
item.description: What all started with a leaked proposal to build golf courses and pickleball courts at state parks last year has now resulted in legislation that aims to protect natural grounds from those very acts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Entire staff at federal agency that funds libraries and museums put on leave
item.description: The staff of the Institute of Museum and Library Services was placed on administrative leave Monday morning, following a meeting between IMLS leadership and DOGE staff.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Limbong, MPR News

item.title: Federal employees union sues over Trump move to block collective bargaining
item.description: A federal employee union has sued to block a Trump administration order that would strip bargaining rights at more than a dozen departments.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: How Biden's frailties hampered Harris
item.description: Democratic Party officials and White House staffers were well aware of former President Biden’s frailties ahead of his choice to end his ill-fated reelection bid last summer, according to exclusive excerpts of a new book released to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Per-pupil funding going up $2 next year unless something changes in Nevada budget
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's proposed state budget sets per-pupil funding in 2026 at $9,416, just $2 higher than the current funding level. Going higher than that is "impracticable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
